scenario_title: delete_rrset for a type that is absent but the owner name exists
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified edge case where the owner name exists, the prerequisite confirms that fact, and the UPDATE requests deletion of a type that is absent. That makes the operation semantically plausible but state-mismatched, which is precisely the gap: some servers may accept it as an idempotent no-op and return NOERROR, while others may reject it with NXRRSET or SERVFAIL. The follow-up queries confirm the post-state is unchanged and provide a consistent observation channel even when only the reply diverges.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset for an absent type as an idempotent no-op when the owner name exists and the prerequisite is satisfied.
group_1.post_state: x.campus.edu. retains its A RRset and has no TXT RRset.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the semantically mismatched delete_rrset as a prerequisite-style or server error while leaving the zone unchanged.
group_2.post_state: x.campus.edu. retains its A RRset and has no TXT RRset.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines delete_rrset as removing all RRs of a given type, but §7.2 does not clearly specify whether attempting to delete an already-absent RRset at an existing name must succeed silently or fail with NXRRSET/SERVFAIL. As a result, compliant servers may differ only in the UPDATE reply code while leaving the zone unchanged.
